channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: In 2025, Trade Deficit in Goods Reached Record High
item.description: Data released Thursday by the Census Bureau showed the overall trade deficit with the world narrowed, the result of an expanding trade surplus in serv...

item.title: Reporter's notebook: My Olympic Lunar New Year
item.description: An NPR reporter covering the Olympics in Milan takes us on cultural side quests, to a hospitality house and a candy store.

item.title: Michael Pollan says AI may 'think' — but it will never be conscious
item.description: "Consciousness is under siege," says author Michael Pollan. His new book, A World Appears, explores consciousness on both a personal and technological...

item.title: American Jordan Stolz speedskates to a third Olympic medal -- silver this time
item.description: U.S. speedskater Jordan Stolz had a lot of hype accompanying him in these Winter Olympic Games. He's now got two gold medals, one silver, with one eve...

item.title: Man Convicted of Murder of 4 Homeless Men in Chinatown
item.description: Randy Rodriguez Santos attacked five homeless men in Chinatown in 2019, killing four of them in a span of minutes.

item.title: With a Golden Gavel and a Threat to Iran, Trump Launches His Board of Peace
item.description: The first gathering of President Trump’s alternative to the United Nations is a manifestation of a Trump World Order.

item.title: Baby chicks link certain sounds with shapes, just like humans do
item.description: A surprising new study shows that baby chickens react the same way that humans do when tested for something called the "bouba-kiki effect," which has ...

item.title: The U.S. and Canada set to square off in Olympic women's ice hockey gold medal match
item.description: Canada was long the top dog of Olympic women's hockey. But with a win Thursday, the Americans could do more than earn a third gold medal — they could ...

item.title: Another Storm Complicates Efforts to Recover Victims of Tahoe Avalanche
item.description: The sheriff’s office in Nevada County, Calif., said a storm that moved through early Thursday has slowed their work.

item.title: A Lonely Baby Monkey Wins Hearts, and Even a Few Friends
item.description: Legions of fans from around the world have been cheering on Punch, a 7-month-old macaque who had been struggling to socialize at a zoo outside Tokyo.

item.title: Live Updates: Former Prince Andrew Is Released After Being Arrested Amid Epstein Revelations
item.description: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was seen leaving police custody on Thursday evening after being detained on suspicions of misconduct in public office. The ...

item.title: Why Is Trump Dumping East Wing Rubble in a Public Park?
item.description: The East Potomac Golf Links is a municipal course that has been a fixture in Washington for decades. President Trump is turning it into something else...

item.title: British Police Arrest Former Prince Andrew Amid Epstein Revelations
item.description: British authorities arrested Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor on Thursday on suspicion of misconduct in public office following the latest release of docume...

item.title: Fans Cheer on Lonely Baby Monkey at Japanese Zoo
item.description: At Japan’s Ichikawa City Zoo, a 7-month-old macaque named Punch has developed an online following after his mother abandoned him because of a difficul...

item.title: King Charles Attends a Fashion Event
item.description: His appearance comes after a statement of support for the investigation into his brother Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor on suspicion of misconduct in publ...
